(born July 31, 1975), known professionally as **Annie Parisse**, is an American actress. She portrayed [Alexandra Borgia](/wiki/Alexandra_Borgia "Alexandra Borgia") on the drama series *[Law & Order](/wiki/Law_%26_Order "Law & Order")*. Parisse has also starred as [Julia Snyder](/wiki/Julia_Snyder "Julia Snyder") on the soap opera *[As the World Turns](/wiki/As_the_World_Turns "As the World Turns")*, for which she was nominated for a [Daytime Emmy Award](/wiki/Daytime_Emmy_Award "Daytime Emmy Award").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American actress (born 1975)

| Annie Parisse | |
| --- | --- |
| Parisse in 2015 | |
| Born | Anne Marie Cancelmi   (1975-07-31) July 31, 1975 (age 50)  [Anchorage](/wiki/Anchorage,_Alaska "Anchorage, Alaska"), [Alaska](/wiki/Alaska "Alaska"), U.S. |
| Alma mater | [Fordham University](/wiki/Fordham_University "Fordham University") |

**Anne Marie Cancelmi** (born July 31, 1975), known professionally as **Annie Parisse**, is an American actress. She portrayed [Alexandra Borgia](/wiki/Alexandra_Borgia "Alexandra Borgia") on the drama series *[Law & Order](/wiki/Law_%26_Order "Law & Order")*. Parisse has also starred as [Julia Snyder](/wiki/Julia_Snyder "Julia Snyder") on the soap opera *[As the World Turns](/wiki/As_the_World_Turns "As the World Turns")*, for which she was nominated for a [Daytime Emmy Award](/wiki/Daytime_Emmy_Award "Daytime Emmy Award").

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Annie_Parisse&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Parisse was born in [Anchorage, Alaska](/wiki/Anchorage,_Alaska "Anchorage, Alaska"), the daughter of Annette, a teacher, and Louis G. Cancelmi, a senior executive with [Alaska Airlines](/wiki/Alaska_Airlines "Alaska Airlines"). She has [Italian](/wiki/Italians "Italians"), [Slovak](/wiki/Slovaks "Slovaks") and [Syrian](/wiki/Syrians "Syrians") ancestry.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-BA-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) Her brother [Louis](/wiki/Louis_Cancelmi "Louis Cancelmi") is also an actor.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Louis is married to Elisabeth Waterston, a daughter of *[Law & Order](/wiki/Law_%26_Order "Law & Order")* veteran and Parisse's co-star [Sam Waterston](/wiki/Sam_Waterston "Sam Waterston").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Parisse grew up in [Mercer Island, Washington](/wiki/Mercer_Island,_Washington "Mercer Island, Washington"),[[3]](#cite_note-BA-3) and attended [Mercer Island High School](/wiki/Mercer_Island_High_School "Mercer Island High School").[[3]](#cite_note-BA-3)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

Parisse moved to New York City when she was 18 to attend [Fordham University](/wiki/Fordham_University "Fordham University"), where she majored in theater and appeared in numerous productions, including *[Medea](/wiki/Medea_(play) "Medea (play)")* and *[Antigone](/wiki/Antigone_(Sophocles) "Antigone (Sophocles)")*, both of which featured her as the lead.[[3]](#cite_note-BA-3)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Annie_Parisse&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

### Television and film

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Annie_Parisse&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Television and film")]

Parisse joined the cast of the [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS") [soap opera](/wiki/Soap_opera "Soap opera") *[As the World Turns](/wiki/As_the_World_Turns "As the World Turns")* as [Julia Snyder](/wiki/Julia_Snyder "Julia Snyder") from 1998 to 2001 and returned for a few episodes in 2002.[[3]](#cite_note-BA-3) She was later nominated for a [Daytime Emmy Award](/wiki/Daytime_Emmy_Award "Daytime Emmy Award") for Outstanding Younger Actress in a Drama Series in 2001.

Early in her career, Parisse guest starred on shows such as *[Big Apple](/wiki/Big_Apple_(TV_series) "Big Apple (TV series)")*, *[Third Watch](/wiki/Third_Watch "Third Watch")*, and *[Friends](/wiki/Friends "Friends")*. She made a 2002 appearance on *Law & Order*, playing an [exotic dancer](/wiki/Exotic_dancer "Exotic dancer") in the episode "Attorney Client", where she testified against a defendant (a [defense attorney](/wiki/Defense_attorney "Defense attorney")) who plotted the murder of his wife. This made her one of the *Law & Order* cast members who had a solo appearance prior to securing a sustaining role, along with [Jerry Orbach](/wiki/Jerry_Orbach "Jerry Orbach"), [S. Epatha Merkerson](/wiki/S._Epatha_Merkerson "S. Epatha Merkerson"), [Michael Imperioli](/wiki/Michael_Imperioli "Michael Imperioli"), [Milena Govich](/wiki/Milena_Govich "Milena Govich"), and [Jeremy Sisto](/wiki/Jeremy_Sisto "Jeremy Sisto").

In a 2002 supporting role, Parisse portrayed Jeannie Ashcroft in *[How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days](/wiki/How_to_Lose_a_Guy_in_10_Days "How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days")*. She also had a minor role in the 2004 action movie *[National Treasure](/wiki/National_Treasure_(film) "National Treasure (film)")*, playing the role of Agent Dawes. For the 2005 film *[Monster-in-Law](/wiki/Monster-in-Law "Monster-in-Law")*, she held a supporting role.

In 2004, Parisse joined the cast of *[Law & Order](/wiki/Law_%26_Order "Law & Order")* during the middle of season 15 as assistant district attorney Alexandra Borgia; she made her final appearance in the May 17, 2006, season 16 finale, in which her character is murdered.

In the 2010 war drama *[The Pacific](/wiki/The_Pacific_(TV_miniseries) "The Pacific (TV miniseries)")*, Parisse took on the role of Marine Sergeant Lena Riggi Basilone, wife of [John Basilone](/wiki/John_Basilone "John Basilone"), the revered [United States Marine](/wiki/United_States_Marine "United States Marine") [Gunnery Sergeant](/wiki/Gunnery_Sergeant "Gunnery Sergeant"). She had a recurring role on CBS' *[Person of Interest](/wiki/Person_of_Interest_(TV_series) "Person of Interest (TV series)")* as Kara Stanton, the former partner of [Jim Caviezel](/wiki/Jim_Caviezel "Jim Caviezel")'s character John Reese. In August 2012, Parisse was cast in a regular role on the [Fox](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Company "Fox Broadcasting Company") series *[The Following](/wiki/The_Following "The Following")*, premiering on January 21, 2013, as an FBI specialist.[[8]](#cite_note-8) In 2016, Parisse appeared in the [HBO](/wiki/HBO "HBO") series *[Vinyl](/wiki/Vinyl_(TV_series) "Vinyl (TV series)")* as Andrea Zito, and in 2017, starred in the [Netflix](/wiki/Netflix "Netflix") series *[Friends from College](/wiki/Friends_from_College "Friends from College")* as Samantha "Sam" Delmonico. In 2018, she appeared in *[Paterno](/wiki/Paterno_(film) "Paterno (film)")* opposite [Al Pacino](/wiki/Al_Pacino "Al Pacino").

### Stage

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Annie_Parisse&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Stage")]

Starting on March 8, 2007, Parisse starred in a revival of [Craig Lucas](/wiki/Craig_Lucas "Craig Lucas")'s *[Prelude to a Kiss](/wiki/Prelude_to_a_Kiss_(play) "Prelude to a Kiss (play)")*, with [Alan Tudyk](/wiki/Alan_Tudyk "Alan Tudyk") and [John Mahoney](/wiki/John_Mahoney "John Mahoney"). In 2008, Parisse reprised her work in an [Off Broadway](/wiki/Off_Broadway "Off Broadway") performance as the title role of *[Becky Shaw](/wiki/Becky_Shaw "Becky Shaw")*.[[9]](#cite_note-9) In the summer of 2011, Parisse appeared in the New York [Public Theater](/wiki/Public_Theater "Public Theater")'s [Shakespeare in the Park](/wiki/Shakespeare_in_the_Park "Shakespeare in the Park") productions of *[Measure for Measure](/wiki/Measure_for_Measure "Measure for Measure")* and *[All's Well that Ends Well](/wiki/All%27s_Well_that_Ends_Well "All's Well that Ends Well")*.

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Annie_Parisse&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Parisse is married to actor [Paul Sparks](/wiki/Paul_Sparks "Paul Sparks"). They have a son, Emmett (born 2009),[[10]](#cite_note-10) and a daughter, Lydia (born 2014). They live in [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan "Manhattan"), [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"), [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)").[[3]](#cite_note-BA-3)

* 2001: Nominated for a [Daytime Emmy Award for Outstanding Younger Actress in a Drama Series](/wiki/Daytime_Emmy_Award_for_Outstanding_Younger_Actress_in_a_Drama_Series "Daytime Emmy Award for Outstanding Younger Actress in a Drama Series") for *[As the World Turns](/wiki/As_the_World_Turns "As the World Turns")*
